what_it_does: Implements a deterministic, restricted rule engine for routing role outputs. Rules are declared as data-ASTs using a closed grammar of operators, validated at construction, evaluated purely against resolved inputs, and routed to one of five destination kinds by a separate driver seam.
observations: Rules are data-AST dict trees with a closed op set in RULE_OPS; no parsing, eval, exec, or compile is used.
The grammar explicitly lacks time, random, IO, call, or wave-order operators, making the whitelist structural rather than a sandbox blacklist.
Validation enforces arity, literal shape, non-empty dot-path field access, and a MAX_RULE_DEPTH of 6.
Rule evaluation is pure: it takes resolved input values and returns a value/decision, with no model calls or side effects.
Per-rule readiness checks that every declared input is resolved, pruned, or failed before evaluation; no global barrier or timeout is used.
Destination kinds are inject, chain, address, surface, and lane; resolve, approve, and dispatch are forbidden verbs.
The Rule dataclass provides addressable records for the live view and one firing record per evaluation.
The route() function is the only place a decision becomes an effect, delegating to store, suite, emit, or a chain executor.
on_missing supports raise or skip; missing inputs never silently default to false.
A clamp operator is included as a layout-allocation primitive.
standouts: Whitelist-by-construction grammar: dangerous capabilities are absent as ops, not runtime-blocked.
Rule.decide() asserts every declared input is present before evaluating the condition, preventing short-circuit hiding of missing references.
route() is structurally prevented from emitting resolve/approve/dispatch; surface reuses Suite.surface_review and emits ask, not resolve.
The lane destination is intentionally minimal and built on the single event log rather than a parallel channel.
conventions: Heavy docstrings with law and decision references.
Registry constants (RULE_OPS, DESTINATION_KINDS, FORBIDDEN_DESTINATION_VERBS) declared as data.
Fail-loud exceptions (RuleError) for malformed rules or missing inputs.
Pure functions separated from effectful driver code.
run:// addressing scheme for durable value landing.
Dataclass with as_record/firing_record for view addressability.
Optional collaborator parameters in route() keep the rule itself effect-free.
concerns: route() collaborators (store, suite, emit, chain_executor) are optional and loosely typed, so runtime misuse can raise RuleError only when a destination needs them.
evaluate() uses Python truthiness implicitly, which may treat 0 or empty collections as false without explicit typing guidance.
_render_ast() collapses min/max/clamp to op(…) placeholders, losing the rendered detail for those ops.
chain_executor returns Any and route() does not define error handling for a failed chained role.
store.put_content and set_ref are assumed but not defined in this file.
The lane destination is noted as under-specified in the comments.
notes: A declarative, closed-grammar rule evaluator that keeps routing decisions pure, validated, and structurally unable to approve or dispatch.
questions: What is the exact interface of store.put_content and store.set_ref assumed by route?
How does the scheduler populate settled with pruned/failed statuses for per-rule readiness?
What is the signature and return shape of Suite.surface_review?
How are G2 descriptive role rules migrated or coexisting with G3 AST rules over time?
Where is the canonical run:// resolver that later reply parts use to read injected values?
How are cognition.lane events consumed and what schema governs lane values?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file implements exactly the declared data-AST rule engine, pure evaluator, per-rule readiness, five destination kinds, and driver seam described in the module docstring.
intention_signals: Replace hand-written Python routing logic with a declarative, auditable rule language that can be statically validated, visually rendered as a short badge, and executed without invoking models, IO, or unsafe evaluation.
novelty: A closed data-AST grammar whose safety comes from missing dangerous ops rather than a runtime sandbox; per-rule readiness without a global barrier; a clamp operator as a layout primitive; and a lane destination that writes typed streams onto a single event log.
contribution: A declarative rule AST, restricted evaluator, validation walk, and routing seam that lets routing decisions be expressed as data, inspected before deployment, and kept free of model execution, IO, and dispatch authority.
summary_for_embedding: runtime/rules.py implements a deterministic, restricted data-AST rule engine with a closed grammar, pure evaluator, per-rule readiness, five routing destinations, and a driver seam that surfaces, injects, chains, addresses, or lanes values without ever emitting resolve, approve, or dispatch.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to replace imperative routing logic with a validated, renderable, pure decision layer that routes role outputs safely by construction.

symbols.does: Exception raised for malformed rules or evaluation errors.
symbols.description: RuleError signals fail-loud conditions during AST validation, rule construction, field lookup, or division by zero. It is used throughout the module to prevent silent routing on bad inputs or malformed declarations.

symbols.does: Checks whether a value is an allowed static literal.
symbols.description: Recursively accepts None, bool, int, float, str, or lists of those. Used by validate_ast to ensure lit nodes contain only frozen, JSON-like values.

symbols.does: Statically validates a rule AST against the closed grammar.
symbols.description: Walks the data-AST recursively, rejecting unknown ops, wrong arities, bad field paths, non-literal lit values, and nesting deeper than MAX_RULE_DEPTH. This is the structural whitelist gate called at commit time.

symbols.does: Collects the first segment of every field path in an AST.
symbols.description: Returns the set of input ids the rule reads. The scheduler uses this set to determine per-rule readiness before evaluation.

symbols.does: Traverses a dot-path by dict-key lookup over resolved values.
symbols.description: Fails loudly if any segment is missing or if an intermediate value is not a dict. It never uses getattr or dunder access, enforcing pure dict-key traversal.

symbols.does: Returns Python bool of a value.
symbols.description: Simple wrapper around bool() used by boolean ops in evaluate. It treats standard Python truthiness as the rule truthiness semantics.

symbols.does: Recursively interprets a data-AST against resolved inputs.
symbols.description: Validates the AST unless pre-validated, then dispatches on op to compute a pure result. It supports boolean, comparison, arithmetic, membership, field, and literal ops without IO or model calls.

symbols.does: Nested helper that evaluates a sub-AST with validation skipped.
symbols.description: Short closure used inside evaluate to recurse into args while passing _validated=True, avoiding repeated full validation of already-validated subtrees.

symbols.does: Dataclass representing one declared routing rule.
symbols.description: Holds id, when AST, destination, params, on_missing policy, and label. Provides construction-time validation, input discovery, readiness checks, pure decision evaluation, and addressable view records.

symbols.does: Validates destination and AST at rule construction.
symbols.description: Ensures the destination is a known kind and not a forbidden verb, on_missing is raise or skip, and the when AST passes validate_ast. A malformed rule raises before it can ship.

symbols.does: Property returning the set of declared input ids.
symbols.description: Exposes declared_inputs(self.when) so callers can inspect the referenceable-input whitelist for scheduling and readiness.

symbols.does: Checks whether all declared inputs are settled.
symbols.description: Returns true only when every input is marked resolved, pruned, or failed in the supplied settled map. Pending inputs make the rule not ready.

symbols.does: Evaluates the rule condition and returns a pure routing decision.
symbols.description: First asserts all declared inputs are present to avoid short-circuit hiding, then evaluates the AST. On missing or deeper errors it either raises or skips based on on_missing. Returns a dict describing fire, value, destination, params, and reason.

symbols.does: Builds a short human-readable reason string.
symbols.description: Combines the rule label or id with the rendered condition text and appends FIRE or no, producing the badge text the view can display.

symbols.does: Renders the condition AST as a short infix string.
symbols.description: Calls _render_ast to produce a readable representation of the when condition for the edge badge, relying on the depth cap to keep it short.

symbols.does: Serializes the rule as addressable view data.
symbols.description: Returns a dict with id, label, when AST, rendered text, destination, params, sorted inputs, on_missing policy, and AST depth. The live view draws from this record.

symbols.does: Records one rule evaluation as addressable data.
symbols.description: Produces a firing record including turn id, rule id, rendered condition, fired flag, destination, reason, and a run:// address for the view.

symbols.does: Renders a data-AST node as a short infix string.
symbols.description: Handles field, lit, not, and a set of infix ops; falls back to op(…) for min/max/clamp. It is a pure formatter, not a parser.

symbols.does: Computes the nesting depth of a data-AST.
symbols.description: Counts depth for operator nodes and returns 1 for leaves. Used by as_record and implicitly bounded by validate_ast.

symbols.does: Constructs and validates a Rule from a declared dict.
symbols.description: Checks required keys id, when, and destination, then instantiates Rule with optional params, on_missing, and label. Serves as the commit-time builder for AST-shaped declarations.

symbols.does: Distinguishes G3 AST rules from older descriptive rule shapes.
symbols.description: Returns true when a declaration has a dict when condition and a destination key, allowing G2 descriptive rules to pass through unchanged.

symbols.does: Statically validates all AST-shaped rules declared by a role.
symbols.description: Iterates a role's rules list, builds and validates AST rules, and re-raises with role context on failure. Non-AST descriptive rules are ignored by this walk.

symbols.does: Performs the effect of a fired routing decision.
symbols.description: The driver seam that acts on a decision: inject lands a value at a run:// address, chain triggers a dependent role via chain_executor, address stores a value, surface reuses Suite.surface_review, and lane emits a cognition.lane event. It never emits resolve, approve, or dispatch.
